reasons: Diggie can protect Yin's intended target during the pull timing before the domain succeeds.
If Yin fails to pull the target, his ultimate becomes a much weaker threat window.

proof.summary: Yin wants to pull one enemy into his domain, while Diggie's Time Journey can give control immunity during the setup window and deny the isolation.
worksBestWhen: Diggie predicts Yin's target and casts before the pull completes.
Diggie stays close enough to protect the ally Yin wants.
failureCases: Yin catches a target away from Diggie.
The domain already succeeds, leaving Diggie outside the isolated fight.

reasons: Melissa's Go Away gives her a strong self-peel answer against melee duelists.
Yin must stay close to finish the isolated duel, which Melissa can punish if her ultimate is timed well.

proof.summary: Yin wants a close-range isolated duel, while Melissa can repel melee enemies with Go Away and keep attacking from safer spacing.
worksBestWhen: Melissa saves Go Away for Yin's all-in.
Melissa has enough damage to punish Yin while he is repelled.
failureCases: Yin baits Go Away before committing.
Melissa is pulled while her ultimate is unavailable.

reasons: Valir can push Yin away before he gets an easy ultimate angle.
His cleanse and movement tools give him a chance to survive Yin's engage timing.

proof.summary: Yin wants to close distance and isolate a target, while Valir can knock him away, slow him, and reset spacing before the duel begins.
worksBestWhen: Valir keeps Searing Torrent for Yin's approach.
Valir avoids standing alone in narrow jungle paths.
failureCases: Yin flanks from fog and reaches Valir before knockback.
Valir wastes disengage on another target.

reasons: Franco can suppress Yin before he casts My Turn or immediately punish him when he returns from the domain.
Yin is weaker if he cannot freely choose his isolation target.

proof.summary: Yin wants to start the fight on his terms, while Franco's suppression can stop him from entering or continuing his combo window.
worksBestWhen: Franco holds Bloody Hunt for Yin instead of using it on tanks.
Franco's team can burst Yin during suppression.
failureCases: Yin casts domain before Franco can react.
Franco is isolated instead of the real carry.

reasons: Kaja can drag Yin away from his intended target before Yin starts the domain fight.
Suppression is a strong answer to dive heroes that depend on one clean all-in window.

proof.summary: Yin wants to isolate a priority target, while Kaja can suppress and drag Yin out of position before Yin completes his pickoff plan.
worksBestWhen: Kaja marks Yin as the main dive threat.
Kaja has teammate burst ready after Divine Judgment.
failureCases: Kaja uses ultimate on a less important target.
Yin waits out Kaja's ultimate before committing.
